title: The Peasant Poet
content:
He loved the brook's soft sound,
    The swallow swimming by.
    He loved the daisy-covered ground,
    The cloud-bedappled sky.
    To him the dismal storm appeared
    The very voice of God;
    And when the evening rack was reared
    Stood Moses with his rod.
    And everything his eyes surveyed,
    The insects in the brake,
    Were creatures God Almighty made,
    He loved them for His sake--
    A silent man in life's affairs,
    A thinker from a boy,
    A peasant in his daily cares,
    A poet in his joy.
meaning:
This evocative piece by John Clare, titled "The Peasant Poet", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
